rationale.completeness: Covers all five required areas (accuracy, per-type breakdown, cost tracking, latency, failure categorization) with dedicated test classes, though some tests are shallow stubs that verify manually duplicated logic rather than the actual module functions.
rationale.correctness: Core tests against real imported functions (check_answer, categorize_failure, normalize_text, load_rubric, evaluate_question) are sound, but calc_stats is duplicated locally instead of tested from source, and test_categorize_failure_reasoning_error documents a known precedence bug rather than asserting correct behavior—raising questions about whether the production logic is actually correct.
rationale.style: Follows standard unittest conventions, uses descriptive class and method names, and imports are organized cleanly; the file header docstring matches the feature description well.
rationale.quality: The test structure is logical with well-separated concerns per class, but several tests (TestCostTracking, TestLatencyMetrics, TestEvaluationMetrics) test trivial arithmetic on local variables rather than exercising real code paths, reducing their value as a safety net; the calc_stats duplication is a maintenance liability.
